goal: Add a repo-safe BP-C1 smoke command and document the BP-C1 Kernel Ready checkpoint before BP-C2 can be prepared.
scope_out: No auth, secrets, DB, deploy, live Builder, AICOS write, or Soulmatch copy
claims: tools/council-kernel-smoke.cjs runs a deterministic Council session in a temporary directory.
The smoke covers init, watcher start, agent registration, task started, task done, event log, session completion, and no committed .bluepilot state.
docs/BP_C1_KERNEL_READY_CHECKPOINT_v0.md states BP-C1 is kernel-ready and BP-C2 may be prepared only under active Council Session rules.
The external manual smoke suggestion is corrected because plain init without a queued task cannot mark TASK_001 done.
assumptions: A deterministic temp-directory smoke is safer than requiring manual multi-terminal repo-local state.
BP-C1 readiness means the local kernel can coordinate queued tasks by file-state changes, not that real parallel Codex processes are spawned.
BP-C2 preparation remains a new WLP task.
stop_conditions: Auth, secrets, DB, deploy, live Builder, AICOS write, or Soulmatch copy becomes necessary
prior_task_findings: BP-089 added a dependency-free watch loop and session init. The remaining BP-C1 gate is a repo-safe smoke/checkpoint proving the kernel path.
